title: taudit Report
description: JSON output from `taudit scan --format json`. Contains the authority graph, all findings, and a summary. Each finding carries a stable `fingerprint` (16 hex chars, SHA-256-derived) byte-identical to the value emitted in SARIF `partialFingerprints[primaryLocationLineHash]` and the CloudEvents extension attribute `tauditfindingfingerprint`. SIEMs key on the fingerprint to deduplicate findings across re-runs.

schema_uri.description: Canonical URI for the schema this document conforms to. Optional for backward compatibility with v1 consumers that pre-date this field.

findings.description: Each entry is a `taudit Finding v1` object — see `https://github.com/0ryant/taudit/schemas/finding.v1.json` (also published in this repo at `schemas/finding.v1.json`). The local `#/$defs/Finding` mirrors that schema for inline validation; the standalone schema lets SIEM consumers validate finding payloads without loading the report wrapper.

rule_id.description: Snake_case rule identifier — custom-rule id when the finding came from a YAML rule with a `[id] ...` message prefix, otherwise the snake_case form of `category`. Byte-identical to SARIF `result.ruleId` and CloudEvents `taudit.rule_id`. JSON consumers filter and group by this field. Optional in v1 for backward-compat; always emitted by current taudit.

source.description: Provenance of the finding. `"built-in"` (string) means the finding was emitted by a rule shipped in the taudit binary. An object `{"custom": {"source_file": "<path>"}}` means the finding came from a custom invariant YAML loaded via `--invariants-dir`; the path is the file the rule was loaded from. Optional in v1 for backward-compat; always emitted by current taudit. SIEMs / triage tooling should treat any non-`built-in` source as untrusted-by-default and surface the originating path in alerts.

fingerprint.description: Stable cross-run identifier for the finding (16 hex chars, SHA-256-derived). Byte-identical to the value emitted in SARIF `partialFingerprints[primaryLocationLineHash]` and CloudEvents `tauditfindingfingerprint`. Use for SIEM dedup and suppression. Optional in v1 for backward-compat; always emitted by current taudit. See docs/finding-fingerprint.md.

finding_group_id.description: Stable UUID v5 derived from the finding fingerprint. Two findings with the same fingerprint share a group id, so SIEMs can collapse N hops against the same authority root into a single advisory. Optional. See docs/finding-output-enhancements.md.

time_to_fix.description: Coarse remediation effort. trivial = ~5 min flag flip; small = ~1 hr step refactor; medium = ~1 day job restructure; large = ~1 week+ ops policy change. Optional; populated per-rule. Triage dashboards sort by `severity * time_to_fix` to surface highest-ROI fixes.

compensating_controls.description: Human-readable list of detected compensating controls that downgraded this finding's severity. Empty when no downgrade applied. Populated by compensating-control detectors (e.g. fork check present, environment gate present, OIDC token used).

suppressed.description: True when a `.taudit-suppressions.yml` waiver matched and the configured mode is `suppress` (vs. `downgrade`). The finding is still emitted so audit trail is preserved; consumers may filter on this field.

original_severity.description: Pre-downgrade severity. Populated whenever a suppression entry OR a compensating control downgraded `severity`. Absent means the current severity is the rule-emitted value.

suppression_reason.description: Operator-supplied justification from the matching `.taudit-suppressions.yml` entry. Absent when no suppression applies.

Recommendation.description: Internally-tagged union. The 'type' field selects the variant; remaining fields are variant-specific.
